title: baloghia drimiflora
intro: Baloghia drimiflora, also known as the "Gardeners' thumb" or "Ugandan bitter leaf", is a plant species in the family Malpighiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Baloghia drimiflora include reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The herbal preparations of Baloghia drimiflora include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be used to treat various ailments.
